description:
This map displays selected Urban Atlas (UA) Land Cover / Land Use (LCLU) classes within the City Perimeter of Leuven, Belgium, overlaid on satellite imagery. The map illustrates the "Final result of selected classes".
*   Dark reddish-brown polygons represent the "UA Urban Areas Classes" (areas where buildings are expected) with an added 50 m buffer to avoid border errors. These areas constitute the main urban fabric, densest in the city centre and along major transportation routes.
*   Orange polygons represent other UA LCLU classes within the City Perimeter, such as green urban areas, railways, and sports and leisure facilities, where buildings are not expected. These features are interspersed throughout the reddish-brown urban areas.
*   A distinct light pink polygon in the north-eastern part of the map indicates a manually created and added missing UA LCLU class polygon within the City Perimeter.
The satellite imagery background shows surrounding rural areas with fields and vegetated patches. No explicit scale bar, compass, or legend is visible on the map.
